reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware inference from stated pressure-dependent trends, rewarding conclusions that separate chemistry effects (LFP vs NCM523) and hazard dimensions (thermal propagation vs venting force) while penalizing mechanistic or safety-overall overclaims. Decoys are largely single-fault and technically plausible, though a couple invite overlapping errors (e.g., conflating propagation time with overall hazard and mixing in temperature comparisons), slightly reducing discriminative sharpness. Overall, it should separate careful experts from heuristic responders without relying on stylistic cues.

The item cleanly targets a boundary-aware safety judgment: longer propagation time at low ambient pressure does not entail improved overall safety because a distinct mechanical venting hazard increases via larger pressure differential. It forces multidimensional risk reasoning and discourages single-metric inference, though as a short-answer item it lacks decoy structure, slightly reducing diagnostic separation and making scoring more rubric-dependent.
